observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) through the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to: (a) adopt appropriate and
legal remedies to enforce the settlement of cash advances granted to officials
and employees, as applicable; (b) file a request for authority to write off all
dormant unliquidated cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-008;
(c) discontinue the granting of cash advances to Accountable Officers that
exceed the maximum accountability limits and if deemed necessary, increase
the fidelity bond premiums of the accountable officers; (d) cease the granting
of special cash advances to undesignated SDOs, and, if necessary, designate
additional SDOs; and (e) submit the lacking documents as enumerated above
and ensure that the appropriate documentary requirements are attached to
the liquidation reports as required by COA Circular No. 2012-001. We
likewise recommended that the LCE through the Municipal Accountant and
OIC-Municipal Treasurer to be prudent and diligent in performing their
functions and ensure that standards in relation to granting, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances are fully and satisfactorily observed and complied
with.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the: (a) Municipal
Accountant to: (i) set-up the RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the year
based on the Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due prepared by the Office
of the Municipal Treasurer; and (ii) stop the practice of recording net
collections only and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the
correct balances of aforesaid accounts; (b) OIC- Municipal Treasurer to: (i)
ensure coordination with the Municipal Assessor for the preparation of a
Certified List of Taxpayers, bearing the names, amount due and collectibles
during the year, and (ii) submit the same to the Accounting Unit as the basis in
setting up of RPT/SET receivables in the succeeding year; and (c) OIC-
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile the records of
their respective offices. We likewise recommended that the LCE through the
Municipal Treasurer to employ effective collection strategies by updating and
posting the notices of delinquency for property taxes at designated areas and
conducting massive campaigns and public information dissemination on the
value of paying taxes; and if warranted, enforce the provision of Section 254
(b) of the RA No. 7160 in the collection of delinquent taxes.

We recommended that the LCE through the: (a) Municipal Accountant
and the Property Officer to: (i) complete and reconcile their respective PPE
records; (ii) exert more efforts in locating documents pertinent to the
acquisition of its PPEs, maintain their respective PPE records, and thereafter,
conduct at least on a quarterly basis, the reconciliation of their PPE
information; and (b) Municipal Accountant to: (i) reclassify tangible assets
costing less than ₱50,000 to the appropriate accounts and prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of aforesaid
accounts; and (ii) comply with the disclosure requirements on the significant
changes and breakdown of material balances of PPE to ensure that relevant
information is available to the intended users of the Financial Statement; and
(c) Disposal Committee to facilitate disposal of unserviceable PPE in
accordance with the prescribed guidelines and procedures. Moreover, it is
reiterated that Management designates a reliable Property Officer or General
Services Officer, supported by a distinct unit with a sufficient number of staff,
to oversee property and supply management.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal
Accountant to: (a) ensure that source agencies are identified, the status of
PPAs assessed, and any unutilized portion of the funds returned to the source
agency, as necessary; and (b) regularly verify, analyze, and validate the
existence of payables, with proper disposition carried out in accordance with
prescribed procedures. We also recommended that Management take
advantage of the one-time cleansing of dormant accounts provided under
COA Circular No. 2023-008 to clear and properly account for long-
outstanding balances.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the: (a) Municipal
Human Resource Management Officer to continue updating and reconciling
leave credits of employees of the Municipality; and (b) Municipal Accountant,
Municipal Budget Officer and MHRMO to develop a structured plan to
integrate the recognition of Leave Benefits Payable within the three-year
transition period as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2024-009.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the MTO and the
BPLO to: (a) strictly enforce compliance with tax assessment requirements by
verifying gross sales declarations against actual financial records and ITR
submissions; (b) proactively conduct examinations of business books of
accounts as mandated by law and the Municipal Revenue Code; and (c)
implement immediate reassessment of businesses that have under-declared
their gross receipts to recover lost revenues.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the: (a) HRMO to: (i)
revise and improve the existing hiring process for JO/COS personnel by
clearly delineating the responsibilities between the HRMO and the Office of
the Mayor; (ii) timely assess the agency’s functions, systems and procedures,
organizational structure, and staffing pattern to figure out the needs and gaps
and to determine the required personnel complement periodically (iii) require
the aforesaid JO/COS personnel who failed to physically report to personally
appear before the AT and present their municipal-issued identification cards
for verification; (b) Municipal Accountant to: (i) ensure completeness of
supporting documents to substantiate all payroll claims; and (ii) immediately
submit the lacking documents, in proper form, to the Office of the Auditor to
facilitate appropriate audit action; and (c) OIC-Municipal Treasurer to
ensure that wages are paid directly to the claimants/payees; otherwise, require
the submission of a written authorization along with valid identification cards
of both the payee and the representative, and attach the same to the paid
payrolls.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal
Accountant to: (a) prepare and submit regularly the Monthly Report of
Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption of Government Motor
vehicles; (b) ensure that all supporting documents are properly accomplished
and transmitted for audit; and (c) establish and implement effective
control/process over the use of government vehicle and fuel consumption as
provided in COA Circular No. 77-61.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the MPDO and
Municipal Engineer to: (a) ensure that the CDP is prepared consistent with
item 3.2.1 of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020; and
(b) strengthen coordination between the planning implementing offices to
ensure technical alignment and adherence to planning standards.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the MPDO, MBO, and
LDRRMO to: (a) strengthen planning and monitoring to optimize the use of
the LDRRMF in line with the LDRRM Plans; and (b) ensure that the PPAs in
the AIP are harmonized with Five-Year DRRMP consistent with Section 12
paragraph 6 of the Republic Act No. 10121 or the Philippine Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Act of 2010.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the GFPS to: (a)
continue adherence with the guidelines set forth under the RA No. 9710 and
PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01; (b) adopt the
parameters of appropriate cost distribution and gender analysis for its PPAs
using the HGDG tools to ensure effective resource allocation and program
implementation; (c) ensure that any changes in the GAD Plan and Budget are
properly reviewed and endorsed by the DILG, in compliance with Section 6 of
the JMC; and (d) establish stricter monitoring and documentation processes to
ensure adherence to regulatory requirements and improve the effectiveness of
gender-responsive initiatives. We likewise recommended that the concerned
officials exercise due diligence and accountability by applying the knowledge
gained from frequent trainings and seminars and consistently adhering to
relevant guidelines in the performance of their duties.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the OIC-Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to: (a) continue the practice of
complying with the withholding and remittance of taxes due the BIR in
accordance with Section 114 (c) of the National Revenue Code as amended by
RA No. 9337 and Revenue Regulation No. 2-98; and (b) perform the
reconciliation and disposition of the outstanding amount and issue payment to
settle the dues, if warranted.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to ensure performance of its
mandate as withholding agent, with emphasis on strict observance of
regulation on timely and intact remittances of employees’ and employers’
contributions in accordance with Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291 dated
May 30, 1997; and (b) ensure to coordinate with GSIS on the reconciliation
and disposition of the outstanding balance.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the OIC-Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to: (a) observe the practice of
remitting Pag-IBIG premiums on a timely basis so that the employees can
enjoy all the privileges of a Pag-IBIG member; and (b) ensure that the balance
of Due to Pag-IBIG account is accurately presented, addressing any
outstanding arrears as necessary.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Accounting and
Treasurer’s Office to: (a) continue the practice of intact and timely
remittances of employees’ and employers’ contributions due to PhilHealth in
accordance with Section 18 of the IRR of the National Health Insurance Act of
2013 and PhilHealth Circular No. 1, s. 2014; (b) ensure that the balance of Due
to PhilHealth account is accurately presented, addressing any outstanding
arrears as necessary.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to continually ensure
observance of applicable rules and regulations in the receipt and use of
government funds and property.

1.9 The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to Management
through Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2025-015(2024)
dated May 7, 2025.
1.10 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Create an Inventory Committee to properly plan and conduct the
physical count of all PPEs to establish the existence, validity, and
accuracy of PPEs and ensure timely and complete submission of the
RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed
deadline;
b. Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant, and OIC-GSO to prepare
complete PPELCs and Property cards and work closely on the
reconciliation and updating of their records. Thereafter, make the
necessary adjustments on any discrepancies noted to present fairly
the balances in the financial statements;
c. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to identify all the properties
that meet the definition of semi-expendable property and make the
necessary adjusting entries;
d. Direct the LAOO III to initiate the processing of the transfer of title
of the four (4) remaining lots under the name of the Government
Agency; and
e. Avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided in COA
Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to finally establish
PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and
accountability.

2.7 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the
OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
a. Draw the necessary entries to transfer the costs of completed projects
identified in CY 2023 and CY 2024 from Construction in Progress
accounts to the appropriate PPE accounts; and
b. Continually coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to monitor the
status of projects charged to the Construction in Progress account,
to ensure that all completed projects are identified for proper
recording and adjustment in the accounting records.

3.11 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Reconstitute the Inventory Committee to perform the following:
• Faithful conduct of the physical count of inventories on a
semestral basis and preparation of the RPCIs for all inventory
accounts;
• Reconciliation of all inventory accounts before the submission of
the RPCI to the Audit Team;
• Inclusion in the Stock Cards, Semi-Expendable Property Cards
and RPCI essential information about the inventories: for the
semi-expendable property: the property custodian, location and
condition; and for drugs and medicines, the expiry dates;
• Strictly comply with the set deadlines in the submission of RPCI;
and
• Facilitate the immediate tagging of all high-valued semi-
expendable property;
b. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
• Strictly apply the Perpetual Inventory Method in recording the
inventories;
• Prepare and maintain the ledgers for all inventory accounts
including the semi-expendable property;
• Reclassify the Drugs and Medicines for Distribution to Drugs
and Medicines Inventory;
• Classify properly the semi-expendable property and record the
issuances of the semi-expendable property as semi-expendable
expenses;
• Ensure that all recorded expenses for the issuances of
inventories are supported with RSMIs/SSMIs; and
• Reconcile their records with those of the GSO and effect
immediately the necessary adjustments or corrections in the
books to bring the inventory accounts to their correct balances;
and
c. Require the OIC-GSO to:
• Prepare and regularly update the stock cards of all inventory
accounts including the semi-expendable property;
• Submit all RSMI/SSMIs of all issued inventories to the OIC-
Municipal Accountant; and
• Reconcile their records with the accounting records and effect
the necessary corrections in their stock cards.

4.7 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the
OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
a. Analyze immediately the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts
and identify the true nature of the transactions for the proper
disposition of the deficiencies;
b. Cause the immediate preparation of reclassification entries for
Philhealth transactions from Due to LGUs to Due to GOCCs;
c. Ensure the proper and complete relevant information in the
supporting schedules of the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts;
and
d. Prepare and make available the supporting schedules of the Due to
NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts for General Fund and Special
Education Fund, and submit the same to the Audit Team.

5.8 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
a. Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
• Perform the review of the Real Property Tax Receivables
account to determine the variances for
corrections/adjustments;
• Conduct regular reconciliation of the Real Property Tax
Receivables balance with the MTO for the accurate recording
and reporting of the account balance; and
• Prepare and maintain the supporting schedule for the Real
Property Tax receivables; and
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to:
• Coordinate with the OIC-Municipal Accountant in the review
of the Real Property Tax Receivables account to determine the
variances for corrections/adjustments; and
• Conduct regular reconciliation of the Real Property Tax
Receivables balance with the OIC-Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the:

OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that perpetual inventory method is observed in the accounting for inventories;

The OIC-General Services Officer to conduct periodic physical count of inventory on hand to pave way for any necessary adjustments in the books; and 

The OIC-Municipal Accountant and the OIC-General Services Officer to maintain the required forms, records and periodic reports for the recognition of expense portion of the inventories and to reconcile the same.

We recommended and  the Local Chief Executive  agreed require the refund of disbursed funds amounting to ₱117,000.00 and henceforth, instruct the concerned officials and employees to immediately stop the granting of additional benefits to Job Order personnel to avoid personal liability for irregular disbursements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to:

Require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to provide the OIC-Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to serve as the basis for the set-up of the correct RPT/SET Receivables in the books; and

Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to review the recording of receivables and reconcile the accounting records with the Municipal Treasurer’s reports and immediately effect the necessary corrections and adjustments.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive and concerned officials agreed to:

Conduct periodic verification and validation of the existence of Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs balances and return to the Source Agency any unexpended amount of Fund Transfer after project completion pursuant to COA Circular No. 94-013; and

Monitor status of implementation of the projects for funds transferred by NGAs and LGUs and ensure that the fund transferred are duly covered by Memorandum of Agreement so as to maximize the benefits derived therefrom.

We recommended and the Management agreed to formulate and implement a doable DRRM Plan with programs, projects and activities adhering with the policies of disaster risk reduction and management to strengthen and enhance the Municipality’s disaster capabilities.

We also recommended and the Management agreed to make proper appropriations and charges against the DRRM fund in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 and other DRRM-related guidelines.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure: 

Strict compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 in recording and booking-up of transactions regarding the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF); 

That all booked transactions are backed-up with actual financial transactions with appropriate supporting documents; and

That the financial statements shall be based on official accounting records kept in accordance with law and the generally accepted accounting principles and standards.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive, the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and all other officials concerned agreed to implement the Municipality’s revised Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan, as well as recommendations given by   the DENR-EMB, once authority is granted in compliance with the provisions of RA No. 9003. 

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive, the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and all other officials concerned agreed to identify and put into effect a permanent and RA No. 9003 compliant alternative to the municipal controlled dumpsite as disposal area for its residual solid wastes.

We further recommended the closure and rehabilitation of the municipal controlled dumpsite in compliance with the provisions of RA No. 9003.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct all officials concerned to furnish the COA Audit Team copies of the perfected contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents within the prescribed period of five (5) days after execution of the same.

We recommended and the Management agreed:

a.	That the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) and Municipal Budget Officer see to it that disbursements charged from LDRRMF are made in accordance with the Municipality’s approved DRRM programs; and

pertinent rules and regulations in the discharge of their fiscal functions
to avoid disallowances on the use of government funds.

a. OIC-Municipal Accountant to:

b. OIC-GSO to:

Maintain stock cards for supplies to track their receipt and disposal, and reconcile them with the accounting unit's ledger cards;
Consolidate weekly the RIS for issued supplies and materials using the SSMI and submit it immediately, along with the RIS, to the Municipal Accountant for recording of the cost of supplies and materials issued; and
Provide a stockroom for the purchased supplies and materials for storage and safekeeping.

Review prior errors in recording the transactions involving the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) and make necessary adjustments; and

No. 9184 and its relevant provisions, so that accurate deductions for the retention money can be made in every progress billings and thus, mitigating the potential risk of losses due to unresolved defects.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive to direct concerned officials and employees to ensure that Purchase Requests, Requests for Quotations and Purchase Orders for meals are duly supported with food specifications to justify the reasonableness of prices indicated therein;

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Municipal Property Inventory and Disposal Committee to (i) complete the physical count of PPEs and render the report on the result thereof in the form and frequency prescribed; (ii) immediately cause the disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with the procedures provided by pertinent regulation; and (iii) recommend the augmentation in the number of members of the Committee to assist in the completion of the physical inventory; (b) Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to (i) prioritize the preparation or reconstruction of PPELC and PC, respectively, for every PPE category; and (ii) regularly reconcile their respective balances; (c) Property Officer to (i) assign property tags and property numbers to each PPE item adopting the prescribed numbering system of COA Circular No. 2020-006;  and (ii) establish accountability on properties through issuance and monitoring of Property Acknowledgement Receipts for PPEs.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) continue the prompt adjustments of valid reconciling items; and (b) request from the concerned depository bank copies of CMs and DMs from when the discrepancies surfaced, to determine the nature and validity of the reported balances, and to effect the necessary adjustments, as warranted.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the General Services Office and the Municipal Accountant to coordinate in the implementation of the Perpetual Inventory Method in the accounting of inventories pursuant to the said Manual.

We commended Management on its adherence with the provisions of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01 and recommended to continue the efficient use of the 20% Development Plan to attain its developmental objectives over time.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Agency Authorized Officer to (a) act promptly on the updating of personnel records to avoid the recurring accumulation of unremitted premiums; (b) coordinate and extend assistance to the GSIS on boosting collection efforts, particularly on unpaid loans of the inactive members; and (c) strictly adhere with the provisions of GSIS Memorandum Circular 03 Series of 2015, on requiring GSIS clearance prior to the granting of terminal leave benefits to avoid the accumulation of unpaid loans.
